ops.title: Claude Code sessions steal fleet plugins into project scope (installed_plugins.json churn); yasyf-home healed via launchd scope-guard 2026-07-13; plugin list shows project-scoped entries as enabled even where they don't load
ops.body:
Claude Code (observed on 2.1.207) intermittently rewrites `~/.claude/plugins/installed_plugins.json` install records for plugins from autoUpdate marketplaces that repos enable in project-level `enabledPlugins` (the cc-guides fleet stamp): a tool/skill-using session REPLACES the entry with `scope: project, projectPath: <its cwd>` — the array does not append. Whichever repo last used them owns them; every other repo's sessions go dark (`agent type 'codex:codex-wrapper' not found`, `Unknown skill: codex`). Trivial `-p "hi"` sessions don't trigger it; steals are intermittent and correlate with plugin use. Diagnosis trap: `claude plugin list` shows a project-scoped plugin as "enabled" from any cwd — enablement ≠ installation; the loader only loads a project-scoped plugin when cwd matches its projectPath.

**yasyf-home fix (2026-07-13):** all six fleet plugins (codex/slop-cop/llm-prompts/writing-docs @skills, cc-notes@cc-notes, captain-hook@captain-hook) re-scoped to user via CLI uninstall/install, plus a launchd guard `com.yasyf.claude-plugin-scope-guard` (WatchPaths + 300s interval) running `~/.claude/plugins/scope-guard/pin-scope-user.py`, which re-asserts a user-scope entry in place (r+ edit preserves the cc-pool 11-way hardlink fan-out — never write via temp+rename). Heal log: `~/.claude/plugins/scope-guard/scope-guard.log`. Remove with `launchctl unload ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.yasyf.claude-plugin-scope-guard.plist`.

Local machine (same version/flags) appends instead of replacing — user entries persist here; the guard is only deployed on yasyf-home. Related: [[cc-pool-config-copies]], [[cc-guides-v3-architecture]] (repo-level enabledPlugins stamp is what exposes the churn), [[model-routing-convention]] (codex lane depends on this plugin loading).
